variants.title: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the tongue smell behind this animal behavior: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.seo_title: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.yt_description: Snakes smell the air with their tongues. Watch the tongue smell first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick.
variants.script: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick. Watch the tongue flick, because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth. That helps snakes track prey, mates, and danger without needing a loud chase. The air is information. Would you notice the trail?
variants.lead: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick. Watch the tongue flick, because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth. That helps snakes track prey, mates, and danger without needing a loud chase. The air is information. Would you notice the trail?

variants.title: Cats groom to reset their scent map
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the grooming signal behind this animal behavior: Cats use whiskers like built-in rulers
variants.seo_title: Cats groom to reset their scent map
variants.yt_description: Cats groom to reset their scent map. Watch the grooming signal first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map.
variants.script: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map. Watch the tongue and paw movements, because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat. variants.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the cloud spiral behind this animal behavior: Storm clouds filter sunlight into a softer glow
variants.seo_title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
variants.yt_description: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine. Watch the cloud spiral first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Storm clouds show where a storm is feeding.
variants.script: Storm clouds show where a storm is feeding. Watch the spiral shape, because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build. Rotation organizes that energy into bands. From above, the storm is not random; it is an engine. Which satellite clue next?
variants.lead: Storm clouds show where a storm is feeding. Watch the spiral shape, because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build. Rotation organizes that energy into bands. From above, the storm is not random; it is an engine. Which satellite clue next?

variants.title: Lightning makes air explode into thunder
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the lightning flash behind this animal behavior: Lightning turns air into a shock wave
variants.seo_title: Lightning makes air explode into thunder
variants.yt_description: Lightning makes air explode into thunder. Watch the lightning flash first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Lightning turns air into a shock wave.
variants.script: Lightning turns air into a shock wave. Watch the flash first, because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast. That air expands suddenly, and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder. The sound is the atmosphere snapping back. Which storm signal next?
variants.lead: Lightning turns air into a shock wave. Watch the flash first, because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast. That air expands suddenly, and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder. The sound is the atmosphere snapping back. Which storm signal next?

variants.hook: Rock layers are time stamps made of stone.

variants.title: Sharks sense tiny electric fields
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the electric sense behind this animal behavior: Sharks can feel electricity in the water
variants.yt_description: Sharks sense tiny electric fields. Watch the electric sense first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Sharks can feel electricity in the water.
variants.script: Sharks can feel electricity in the water. Watch the head before the turn, because sharks have sensory pores that detect tiny electric fields from other animals. That helps them hunt when sight is limited. The ocean is full of signals we cannot feel. Would you swim differently?

variants.title: Dragonflies lock onto prey in midair
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the motion lock behind this animal behavior: Dragonflies track prey with extreme precision
variants.seo_title: Dragonflies lock onto prey in midair
variants.yt_description: Dragonflies lock onto prey in midair. Watch the motion lock first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Dragonflies track prey with extreme precision.
variants.script: Dragonflies track prey with extreme precision. Watch the eyes before the flight path, because dragonflies process motion fast. They can predict where prey is going, then adjust in the air like a tiny interceptor. That is why the catch looks impossibly clean. Would you see it coming?
variants.lead: Dragonflies track prey with extreme precision. Watch the eyes before the flight path, because dragonflies process motion fast. They can predict where prey is going, then adjust in the air like a tiny interceptor. That is why the catch looks impossibly clean. Would you see it coming?
